query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Corpulence', 'The state or characteristic of being large, plump', 'Her corpulence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corpulent', 'Large in body, fat, overweight', 'What seemed corpulent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corpuscle', 'A minute particle, an atom, a molecule', 'Scholars noted a striking corpuscle between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Correlate', 'To relate things having corresponding characteristics', 'Critics argued that the plan might correlate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Correlation', 'A connection or relationship between two things', '“There is a correlation between diet and health.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Correlative', 'Mutually related, corresponding', 'The passage presents a correlative portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corrigible', 'Able to be corrected or set right', 'The author hoped the new policy would corrigible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corroborate', 'To confirm or support with evidence', '“The witness corroborated the suspect’s alibi.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corroboration', 'The act of strengthening or confirming, addition of strength', 'The essay highlights the corroboration of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corrode', 'To eat away by degrees, to wear away or diminish gradually', 'The author hoped the new policy would corrode tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corrosion', 'The process of eating away by degrees', 'Her corrosion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corrosive', 'Having the power of gradually wearing, or destroying matter', 'The passage presents a corrosive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corrugate', 'Shaped into alternating ridges and grooves', '“The corrugated metal roof was rusted.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corruptible', 'Showing a willingness to act dishonestly', 'Over time, the community learned to corruptible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Corruption', 'The act of corrupting or of impairing integrity', 'The essay highlights the corruption of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmetic', 'External or superficial, pertaining only to the surface', 'The author hoped the new policy would cosmetic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmic', 'Of or from or pertaining to the cosmos or universe', 'The author hoped the new policy would cosmic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmo', 'The universe seen as a well-ordered whole', 'Scholars noted a striking cosmo between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmogony', 'A branch of science dealing with the origin of the universe', 'Scholars noted a striking cosmogony between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmography', 'The creation of maps of the universe', 'Scholars noted a striking cosmography between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmology', 'The study of the physical universe, its structure, etc', 'The essay highlights the cosmology of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmopolitan', 'Familiar with and at ease in many different countries', 'Her cosmopolitan became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosmopolitanism', 'The idea that all people belong to a single moral community', 'The author hoped the new policy would cosmopolitanism tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cosset', 'To care for and protect in an overindulgent way', 'The author hoped the new policy would cosset tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coterie', 'A circle of people who associate with one another', 'Scholars noted a striking coterie between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Countenance', 'A person''s face or facial expression', 'Her countenance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counter-claim', 'A claim  made to offset another claim', 'Scholars noted a striking counter-claim between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counteract', 'To oppose or neutralize the effects of something by taking contrary or opposing actions', 'The author hoped the new policy would counteract tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counterbalance', 'A weight that balances another weight', 'The essay highlights the counterbalance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Countercharge', 'A charge by an accused person against their accuser', 'Scholars noted a striking countercharge between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counterfactual', 'Relating to or expressing what has not happened or is not the case', 'Over time, the community learned to counterfactual its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counterfeit', 'To falsely produce what appears to be official or valid', 'The author hoped the new policy would counterfeit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Countermand', 'To rescind by giving an order contrary to a previous one', 'Critics argued that the plan might countermand the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counterpart', 'Either of two parts that fit together or are complements', 'Her counterpart became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counterproductive', 'Having the opposite of the desired effect', 'The reviewer described the tone as counterproductive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Countervail', 'To have the same value as', 'Over time, the community learned to countervail its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Counting-house', 'The facility in which a business carries out its operations', 'The essay highlights the counting-house of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coup', 'A highly successful, unexpected act', 'Her coup became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Courageous', 'Of a person, displaying or possessing bravery', 'The passage presents a courageous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Course', 'To run or flow, especially of liquids', 'Critics argued that the plan might course the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Courser', 'A person who hunts with dogs using sight rather than scent', 'Scholars noted a striking courser between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Courtesy', 'Given or done as a polite gesture', 'Scholars noted a striking courtesy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Covenant', 'An agreement between two or more persons, like a pact', 'Her covenant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Covert', 'Not openly acknowledged or displayed', 'The reviewer described the tone as covert, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Covet', 'To yearn to possess or have (something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would covet tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Covey', 'A small group of people or things', 'Scholars noted a striking covey between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cower', 'To crouch or cringe in fear', 'Over time, the community learned to cower its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coxswain', 'A person in charge of a ship''s boat and its crew', 'Her coxswain became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coy', 'Artfully or affectedly shy or reserved', 'Her coy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cozen', 'To cheat, deceive or trick', 'The author hoped the new policy would cozen tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
